master_reasoning: Valid suggestion: `dirname(configFilePath)` on a workspace-relative path produces a relative path, so importing `resolve` to anchor against `workspaceRoot` is a real correctness improvement.
Valid: strict `JSON.parse` would reject `.oxlintrc.json` files with comments/trailing commas (common oxlint practice), causing the plugin to skip valid projects.
Valid CWD-dependence concern: `relative(workspaceRoot, dirname(configFile))` mixes absolute and relative paths; resolving `projectRootAbs` first is correct.
Valid: typescript-preset also needs `resolve` from `node:path` for the same CWD-independent path handling reasons.
Valid: `relative(workspaceRoot, projectRoot)` in `shouldSkipPath` is CWD-dependent when `projectRoot` is relative; resolving first is correct.
Valid: `join(projectRoot, name)` on a relative `projectRoot` is CWD-dependent; resolving against `workspaceRoot` first is the right fix.
Valid CWD concern: `relative(workspaceRoot, resolve(workspaceRoot, projectRoot))` is redundant and CWD-dependent; using `projectRoot` directly is cleaner.
Valid: hand-written `plugin.d.ts` is redundant when `plugin.ts` already has full types and `tsdown` with `dts: true` generates output declarations.
Critical real bug: the original `**/biome.json` glob would never match `biome.jsonc` projects, silently dropping them from inference; the fix to `**/biome.jsonc?` is correct and important.

master_reasoning: Valid for the original PR head: `plugins: []` would have prevented Nx from loading any of the four plugins, defeating the PR's purpose; now fixed by registering entry points.
Critical real bug: passing `OXLINT_RC_PATTERN.source` (a regex literal string) as the Nx glob pattern would never match any file, making the plugin non-functional.
Valid CWD-dependence concern in `shouldSkipPath`; the resolve-first fix in 6914393 addresses it.
Valid: the spec explicitly required both `pluginsConfig` entries AND `plugins` array entries, but the wording could mislead readers; the maintainer clarified intent.
Same root cause as 3448758023 — real bug where `biome.jsonc` projects would be silently excluded from target inference.

Valid design issue: the hardcoded `**/tsconfig.json` glob means the `configFile` option is a no-op for any value other than `tsconfig.json`; users setting it to e.g. `tsconfig.lib.json` get zero projects.
Same as 3448755926: real bug in the pre-fix version where `#!/usr/bin/env bun` would fail to execute shell commands; now fixed with `#!/usr/bin/env sh`.
Same CWD-dependence concern as 3448756924; valid finding now addressed in 6914393.
Same as 3448756922: valid concern about strict JSON.parse rejecting valid oxlint configs with comments/trailing commas.

Critical: pre-commit formatting the entire repo (`.`) and not re-staging would modify unrelated files and drop formatting changes from the commit; `--staged` + `git update-index --again` is the correct fix.
Critical real bug: oxlint's default `plugins` (eslint, oxc) provide core rules like no-unused-vars; overwriting without including them would silently drop essential lint coverage.
Premise is wrong for the post-merge repo: per-package `vitest.config.ts` files are discovered by `bun run --filter`, so tsdown specs do run; the maintainer's reply correctly notes this.
Valid: README claimed `clean` "prefixes `--build --clean`" but the implementation actually runs a separate command chained with `&&`; documentation now matches behavior.
